rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself within diffusion-of-innovation research, uses country-level diffusion modeling across seven countries and multiple generations, and asks cross-country diffusion/profitability questions. The cited paper is a core global diffusion study (162 countries) introducing a coupled-hazard approach and country-level drivers of diffusion timing, which could plausibly serve as a foundational or comparison reference rather than a purely incidental mention. With this thematic and methodological proximity, it is not safe to treat as obvious mention-only.
